title: Transmission electron microscopy protocol for anaerobic ciliates

steps: Isolate 1 ml of thriving culture of ciliate cells, fix with 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde (Polysciences) and subsequently centrifuge at 800×g for 5 min at 4 °C.
Carefully replace supernatant with 1 ml of 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde in 0.2M SCB (Sodium Cacodylate buffer, pH 7.2) and incubate 1 hour on ice.
Then, rinse the pellet carefully with SCB three times per 15 minutes.
Postfix the cells with 1% OsO4 in distilled water for 1 hour on ice.

Rinse the cells with distilled water (5 minutes), dehydrate them in a graded ethanol series from 30% to absolute ethanol: through 30%, 50%, 70%, 80%, 90%, 95%, and finally three times in absolute ethanol, each per eight minutes.
Let the embedded cells get polymerized at 70 °C for 48 hours.

Cut serial ultrathin sections on an Ultracut E ultramicrotome (Reichert) using a diamond knife and stain with lead citrate and uranyl acetate (2–3%). Protocol is adapted from Rotterova et al. (2018) and Beinart et al. (2018). Tropidoatractidae fam. nov., a Deep Branching Lineage of Metopida (Armophorea, Ciliophora) Found in Diverse Habitats and Possessing Prokaryotic Symbionts.. Protist. The genome of an endosymbiotic methanogen is very similar to those of its free-living relatives.. Environmental microbiology.
Incubate cells two times in 1:1 ethanol-acetone mixture per 15 minutes.
Incubate cells once in 2 ml of 100% acetone per 15 minutes, and finally substitute aceton with acetone-resin mixture (EPON resin Poly/Bed 812, Polysciences) and incubate per 6-12 hours.
Substitute absolute EPON resin (Poly/Bed 812, Polysciences) three times and finally embed cells in in the resin into capsules.
creator: Johana Rotterova
